perturbation.original_text: The Copernicus Accelerator programme is comprised of different phases, for a total coaching support of approximately one year , aimed at helping Participants´ Ideas achieving measurable results.
perturbation.changed_text: The Copernicus Accelerator programme may or may not be comprised of different phases, for a total coaching support that could be shorter or longer than one year, and that is not necessarily aimed at helping Participants' Ideas achieving measurable results.
perturbation.explanation: By introducing uncertainty ('may or may not', 'could be shorter or longer', 'not necessarily aimed at') regarding the structure, duration, and goals of the program, this change directly contradicts the later specifics in section 2 regarding the structure of the coaching service.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Procedure and Terms of Participation The Copernicus Accelerator programme is comprised of different phases, for a total coaching support of approximately one year , aimed at helping Participants´ Ideas achieving measurable results. When submitting the request to become Mentor, Mentor needs to specify in which fields he/she can support Participants, in order to allow a suitable matchmaking with the requests and needs communicated by Participants. When a Mentor is requested by a Participant of the programme, he/she can access the Participant's profile and examine the submitted idea, in order to decide whether to confirm the request or not. Mentors can only advise up to two participants. Mentors will meet the Participants at the Accelerator Bootcamp , that will take place in line with the Awards Ceremony of the Copernicus Masters , the European Satellite Navigation Competition (ESNC) , and the accompanying Satellite Masters Conference from 6 - 8 November 2017 in Tallinn , Estonia . Mentor´s participation to this event is mandatory, as it will set forth the official start of their relationship: Mentor and Participants will begin developing a coaching plan together, that will include objectives and KPIs of their affiliation. Monthly v irtual interactions need to be planned by Mentor, to ensure a continuous acceleration of the Participants Ideas ´ towards business maturity. A mid -term review and survey will be required by April 2018, to evaluate progress made and ensure that the recommend ations of the coaching plan are currently implemented by Participants. In case of a non -positive matching or non-compliance to his/her assigned duties (having monthly interactions with the mentees, keeping contacts with the Copernicus Accelerator team , promptly informing the organizers about any relevant issue connected to the coaching relationship) , Mentor may be replaced by a more suitable candidate for the Participants´ needs. Mentor will actively support Participants until Ju ly 201 8, and will then submit a final report together, detailing the outcome of the coaching experience. The Organiser will continuously monitor all relationships, in order to assist both parties and guarantee a positive outcome of the programme. Mentor ne eds to deliver all requested reports and schedule all requested meetings. The Organiser will provide Mentor with templates for the documents to be produced. Mentors and Participants will meet again at the closing bootcamp, held in October/November 2018, th at will official seal the closing of their mentoring relationship. At the end of the coaching service, Mentor will fill in a questionnaire on the coaching service and the overall experience and will also be subject to a specific questionnaire compiled by Participants.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces uncertainty about the Copernicus Accelerator program's structure, duration, and goals ('may or may not', 'could be shorter or longer', 'not necessarily aimed at'). This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which describes the program as having 'different phases', 'approximately one year' of coaching support, and being 'aimed at helping Participants´ Ideas achieving measurable results'. The contradicted section relies on these specifics to outline the mentorship roles, timelines, and expectations, which are undermined by the uncertainty introduced in the changed text, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Mentor´s participation to this event is mandatory, as it will set forth the official start of their relationship: Mentor and Participants will begin developing a coaching plan together, that will include objectives and KPIs of their affiliation.
perturbation.changed_text: Mentor´s participation to this event is optional, and even if the Mentor participates, there is no requirement to develop a coaching plan together with Participants, nor to define objectives and KPIs.
perturbation.explanation: This change makes the Bootcamp participation optional and removes the requirement for a coaching plan, directly conflicting with the clear mandatory nature established in the original statement. This creates an in-text contradiction within the procedure section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Procedure and Terms of Participation The Copernicus Accelerator programme is comprised of different phases, for a total coaching support of approximately one year , aimed at helping Participants´ Ideas achieving measurable results. When submitting the request to become Mentor, Mentor needs to specify in which fields he/she can support Participants, in order to allow a suitable matchmaking with the requests and needs communicated by Participants. When a Mentor is requested by a Participant of the programme, he/she can access the Participant's profile and examine the submitted idea, in order to decide whether to confirm the request or not. Mentors can only advise up to two participants. Mentors will meet the Participants at the Accelerator Bootcamp , that will take place in line with the Awards Ceremony of the Copernicus Masters , the European Satellite Navigation Competition (ESNC) , and the accompanying Satellite Masters Conference from 6 - 8 November 2017 in Tallinn , Estonia . Mentor´s participation to this event is mandatory, as it will set forth the official start of their relationship: Mentor and Participants will begin developing a coaching plan together, that will include objectives and KPIs of their affiliation. Monthly v irtual interactions need to be planned by Mentor, to ensure a continuous acceleration of the Participants Ideas ´ towards business maturity. A mid -term review and survey will be required by April 2018, to evaluate progress made and ensure that the recommend ations of the coaching plan are currently implemented by Participants. In case of a non -positive matching or non-compliance to his/her assigned duties (having monthly interactions with the mentees, keeping contacts with the Copernicus Accelerator team , promptly informing the organizers about any relevant issue connected to the coaching relationship) , Mentor may be replaced by a more suitable candidate for the Participants´ needs. perturbation.justification: The original text states "Mentor´s participation to this event is mandatory" and "Mentor and Participants will begin developing a coaching plan together", while the changed text makes participation optional and removes the requirement for a coaching plan. The contradicted text contains the original mandatory participation and coaching plan requirement. Therefore, the changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text, creating a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, where conflicting implications arise from a clause being placed in multiple locations, creating uncertainty about obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The coaching service provided by Mentor is worth a maximum fee of EUR 5,000 (VAT excluded) per Participant coached , that will be remunerated to Mentor at the end of the programme, after the submission and approval of the final summary report of the coaching relationships.
perturbation.changed_text: The coaching service provided by Mentor may or may not be worth a fee, and the amount is subject to change without notice. The timing and conditions for payment are also at the discretion of the Organizer.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces significant uncertainty regarding compensation for the mentor's services, contradicting the defined fee and payment terms outlined in the original clause. This undermines the enforceability of the payment clause and creates potential disputes. The contradiction arises because the original text clearly states the maximum fee and the timing of the payment, while the change introduces ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Payment The Organiser will reimburse Mentor the travel arrangements and accommodation for the Acceleration Bootcamp in Tallinn in 2017 (a maximum of EUR 800) and the closing bootcamp in 2018 (a maximum of EUR 700) . The coaching service provided by Mentor is worth a maximum fee of EUR 5,000 (VAT excluded) per Participant coached , that will be remunerated to Mentor at the end of the programme, after the submission and approval of the final summary report of the coaching relationships. Payment shall be made within 30 days from the receipt of invoice, and following the invoice verification by Organiser. No deductions w ill be made from the gross fees paid to mentor who shall be solely responsible for ensuring that all and any Government taxes and other deductions are paid by him/her.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a maximum fee of EUR 5,000 and defines the timing of payment. The changed text introduces uncertainty by stating that the coaching service 'may or may not be worth a fee' and that the amount is 'subject to change without notice', directly contradicting the original text's defined fee and payment terms. The 'Contradicted Text' explicitly states that 'The coaching service provided by Mentor is worth a maximum fee of EUR 5,000 (VAT excluded) per Participant coached'. The modified clause undermines this defined fee, creating a strong in-text contradiction according to the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information does not include: > information already known or independently developed by Mentor prior to the disclosure of any Idea; > information already in the public domain through no wrongful act of Mentor; or > information received from a third party who was free to disclose such information. In case of doubt any information is deemed to be Confidential Information unless Mentor proves that such information is not confidential.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information includes all information, regardless of whether it was previously known to the Mentor, in the public domain, or received from a third party. All information is confidential, even with proof otherwise.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that 'Confidential Information includes all information, regardless...' and 'All information is confidential, even with proof otherwise', the definition of 'Confidential Information' directly conflicts with the original exclusions, undermining the limitations to confidentiality and making it overly broad and potentially unenforceable. This creates a contradiction because the change nullifies the previous conditions for what is NOT considered confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definition of Confidential Information “Confidential Information ” means any Idea di sclosed to Mentor, all data and information, know -how, business concepts, software, proce dures, products, services, development projects, and programmes contained in such Idea and/or its description and any conclusions. Confidential Information does not include: > information already known or independently developed by Mentor prior to the disclosure of any Idea; > information already in the public domain through no wrongful act of Mentor; or > information received from a third party who was free to disclose such information. In case of doubt any information is deemed to be Confidential Information unless Mentor proves that such information is not confidential.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that 'Confidential Information includes all information, regardless...' and 'All information is confidential, even with proof otherwise'. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which defines Confidential Information and then explicitly excludes certain types of information, such as information already known to the Mentor or in the public domain. The change eliminates these exclusions, creating a direct conflict and a strong in-text contradiction as it nullifies the limitations to confidentiality outlined in the contradicted text. This aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' because the changed text causes a conflicting implication compared to the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be effective as of 2 May 2017 and may not be terminated except for important cause.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be effective as of 2 May 2017 and may be terminated by either party at any time, for any reason, with or without notice.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states the agreement 'may not be terminated except for important cause'. The modified clause states the agreement 'may be terminated by either party at any time, for any reason, with or without notice.' This directly contradicts the 'may not be terminated except for important cause' aspect of the original text. This in-text contradiction introduces significant uncertainty and fundamentally alters the nature of the agreement, from one with restricted termination to one with free termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term This Agreement shall be effective as of 2 May 2017 and may not be terminated except for important cause. Notwithstanding the termination of this Agreement , any Confidential Information must be kept confidential for as long as such Confidential Information is not publicly known unless it becomes part of the public domain through no wrongful act of Mentor. This agreement may not be changed or modified, except by an agreement in writing , signed by both parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement 'may not be terminated except for important cause', while the changed text allows termination 'by either party at any time, for any reason, with or without notice'. This directly contradicts the conditionality of termination in the original text and the contradicted text, which contains the same clause. The change allows for termination without 'important cause', creating a clear contradiction and uncertainty about the conditions under which the agreement can be terminated. The provided explanation accurately states this contradiction as well.
